mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate positive from negative return days with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST yields positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, which directly supports earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle periods, with a negative or zero Rank IC over a 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of cases, or the threshold condition shows no predictive alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the signal generates negative alpha in >60% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions and weakening manufacturing demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks are followed by positive 5-day returns in >70% of cases, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.
